title: BCIT Advanced Guard Duty
description: The Advanced Guard Duty dataset comprises EEG, eye-tracking, and behavioral data collected during a sustained vigilance task involving verification of replica identification cards. Participants performed a 50-minute guard duty task organized into ten 5-minute blocks with varying ID presentation rates (1-25 per minute) and verification criteria. The study was designed to measure cognitive workload and fatigue in realistic vigilance settings, with data collected from a 256-channel BioSemi EEG system synchronized with eye-tracking and driving simulator vehicle dynamics.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using a BioSemi 256-channel system (plus 8 additional channels including 4 eye and 2 mastoid channels) at 1024 Hz sampling rate. Eye tracking was performed with a Sensomotoric Instruments REDEYE250 system. Participants completed a guard duty task involving serial presentation of replica ID cards paired with reference face images from the Multi-PIE database. The task consisted of ten 5-minute blocks with ID presentation rates varying stochastically between 1 and 25 per minute according to predefined profiles. Participants made forced-choice allow/deny decisions with a maximum response time of 20 seconds per trial.
